objective: Configure the remote GitHub MCP server
prompt: Which URL configures GitHub's remote MCP server?

options.text: https://mcp.github.dev/server/
https://api.github.com/copilot/mcp/
https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/
https://github.com/mcp/api/

explanation: GitHub documents https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ as the remote GitHub MCP server endpoint.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Setting up the GitHub MCP Server

prompt: An enterprise wants Copilot users to run only MCP servers published in its approved registry. Which policy should it select?

options.text: Registry only
Signed commits only
Local servers only
Allow all

explanation: The Registry only policy limits MCP use to servers represented in the configured registry. GitHub notes current enforcement limitations, so highly sensitive organizations may still choose to disable MCP entirely.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Configure MCP server access

prompt: Before enabling an agent in CI, which two context checks are essential? Select two.

options.text: The agent's preferred prose style
The repository, branch or commit, and working-directory scope
The number of emojis in its status messages
Available permissions, secrets, network access, and installed dependencies

explanation: Reliable execution depends on knowing exactly what code state the agent is operating on and what capabilities the environment supplies. Style preferences do not replace repository and environment validation.

objective: Use isolated branches and pull requests
prompt: Why is branch-scoped execution with a pull request safer than allowing an agent to write directly to the default branch?

options.text: It prevents the agent from reading repository files
It eliminates the need for human review
It guarantees that generated code is correct
It makes the diff reviewable and keeps branch protections and required checks in the control path

explanation: Copilot cloud agent works on a constrained branch and remains subject to repository protections and checks. A pull request exposes the diff and review evidence; it does not guarantee correctness or remove human responsibility.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Risks and mitigations for Copilot cloud agent

prompt: Which failure is the best candidate for an automatic bounded retry?

options.text: A policy hook explicitly denying the operation
A transient HTTP 503 from an external tool
A malformed migration plan
A repeated authentication failure caused by a missing credential

explanation: Transient service failures can often be retried with a limit and backoff. Policy denials, invalid plans, and persistent authentication problems require correction or escalation rather than repetition.

prompt: A developer rewinds a Copilot CLI session to an earlier workspace snapshot. Which two effects should they expect? Select two.

options.text: Manual edits made after that snapshot are reverted
Only files explicitly edited by Copilot are reverted
The current conversation remains unchanged
New files created after the snapshot are removed

explanation: Copilot CLI rewind restores the whole workspace and truncates later session history. It can revert manual edits and shell-command effects, and it removes files that did not exist at the selected snapshot.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Canceling and rolling back Copilot CLI

prompt: An agent's build needs an external schema registry, but outbound network access is intentionally blocked. What is the best response?

options.text: Tunnel traffic through an unrelated public service
Use an approved cached artifact or request a narrowly scoped allowlist change through the controlled process
Embed a long-lived administrator credential in the repository
Disable the network policy

explanation: Environment constraints are part of the agent's execution contract. The safe response is to use an approved offline input or request the minimum controlled network exception—not bypass policy or expose credentials.

prompt: Which two records best support accountability for an agent's external tool call? Select two.

options.text: The full plaintext credential used for authentication
A link from the call to the workflow run, commit, issue, or pull request that authorized it
Tool name, timestamp, sanitized arguments, result status, and correlation ID
The agent's hidden chain of thought

explanation: Accountability requires attributable, reviewable metadata and a link to the authorizing workflow context. Hidden reasoning is not audit evidence, and credentials must never be written into logs or artifacts.
